headline: Sarawak Demands Return of Education Autonomy
context: GPS lawmakers introduced motion in March 2026 demanding state control over primary and secondary education curricula under MA63 provisions.

cards.big: Sarawak GPS demands state control over school curricula under MA63
cards.sub: Motion argues federal education system ignores Borneo history and languages

cards.big: Only 2 of 48 chapters in Form 5 history textbook cover Borneo role
cards.sub: Sarawak educators say Peninsula-centric curriculum alienates East Malaysian students

cards.big: Sarawak has 1,200 schools but only 45 use indigenous languages as medium
cards.sub: State wants authority to mandate bilingual education in Iban Bidayuh and Melanau

cards.big: Education Ministry spends RM340 per Sarawak student versus RM520 per KL student
cards.sub: Funding disparity persisted for 15 years despite multiple complaints to Parliament

cards.big: Education autonomy tests whether Borneo children see themselves in their own curriculum
Education autonomy was promised in 1963. Sixty-three years later, Sarawak still waits for the handover.
